prompt: top-down arena shooter, players shoot each other for points
contract_warnings: Health is tracked client-side via playerHealthMap updated from server messages; slight desync may occur under latency
Bullet visuals are client-predicted from bullet_fired broadcast; actual hit resolution is fully server-side
player_killed includes newScore for the shooter — network must call ctx.scoreState.set(shooterId, newScore) in that handler

specialists.specialist_description: Manages Colyseus game_room connection, all message sending/receiving, ctx.scoreState writes, ctx.localPlayerId assignment. Routes incoming server messages to stacked onMessage handlers. Listens for localPlayerMoved and shootRequested events to relay to server.
Creates the top-down arena environment: ground plane, boundary walls, and scattered obstacle blocks. Exposes arena dimensions and wall collision bounds for movement clamping and bullet collision.

Spawns and manages player entities (local + remote). Handles WASD movement with arena-bound clamping, mouse-aim rotation, health tracking. Registers onMessage handlers for player_joined, player_left, player_moved, player_hit, player_killed, and state_snapshot to update remote player meshes and health.

Handles click-to-shoot input, spawns and animates bullet visuals, plays hit/death effects. Registers onMessage handlers for bullet_fired (spawn remote bullets), player_hit (hit sparks), and player_killed (death explosion). Emits shootRequested for network relay.
Renders the live multiplayer leaderboard from ctx.scoreState sorted highest-first, per-player health bars floating above player meshes, a crosshair overlay, and a kill-feed notification log.

ctx_extensions.name: scoreState
ctx_extensions.description: Server-authoritative player scores. Written ONLY by network module via scoreState.set(). Key=playerId, Value=current kill count.

ctx_extensions.name: localPlayerId
ctx_extensions.description: Session ID of the local player, set by network in build(). Other modules read in start() or later.

ctx_extensions.name: players
ctx_extensions.type: Map<string, { mesh: BABYLON.Mesh, x: number, z: number, rotation: number, health: number, alive: boolean }>
ctx_extensions.description: All active player entities with their meshes, positions, and health. Managed exclusively by player_controller.

ctx_extensions.name: arenaConfig
ctx_extensions.type: { width: number, depth: number, wallMeshes: BABYLON.Mesh[], wallBounds: Array<{ minX: number, maxX: number, minZ: number, maxZ: number }>, spawnPoints: Array<{ x: number, z: number }> }
ctx_extensions.description: Arena geometry data: overall size, wall meshes for rendering, wall axis-aligned bounding boxes for collision, and spawn point locations.

ctx_extensions.name: playerHealthMap
ctx_extensions.description: Current health per player. Updated by player_controller when receiving player_hit and player_killed messages. Max health is 100.

mesh_registry.name: arena_floor
mesh_registry.description: Ground plane mesh for the arena. Used for pointer picking (aim target).

mesh_registry.name: arena_walls
mesh_registry.description: Array of wall/obstacle meshes for collision checks.

mesh_registry.name: player_body
mesh_registry.description: Template mesh for a player character. Cloned per player. Cylinder with a gun-barrel direction indicator.

mesh_registry.name: bullet_template
mesh_registry.description: Small emissive sphere mesh used as template. Cloned per active bullet.

client_to_server.description: Sends local player's current position and rotation, throttled to sync_rate_hz.
Player fires a bullet from their position in the aimed direction.

server_to_client.description: Full game state sent to a newly joined client. Network module iterates players to set scoreState entries.

payload_shape.players: Array<{ playerId: string, x: number, z: number, rotation: number, score: number, health: number }>

server_to_client.description: A new player connected. Network sets scoreState.set(playerId, 0).
A player disconnected. Network calls scoreState.delete(playerId).
Relayed position update for a remote player.
Server validated bullet. All clients spawn the visual.
A bullet hit a player. Contains shooter's updated score. Network calls scoreState.set(shooterId, newScore).
A player was killed and will respawn. Contains shooter's updated score. Network calls scoreState.set(shooterId, newScore).

win_conditions: First player to reach 10 kills wins the round
fail_conditions: None — continuous respawn, no permanent elimination

visual_spec.camera: Fixed top-down camera (ArcRotateCamera, alpha=0, beta=0, radius=25) looking straight down, attached to local player with smooth follow
visual_spec.arena_style: Dark metallic rectangular floor (20x20 units) with neon-edged boundary walls and 4-6 scattered rectangular obstacle blocks
visual_spec.player_style: Colored cylinder (radius 0.4, height 0.3) with a thin box extending forward as gun barrel. Each player assigned a unique bright color.
visual_spec.bullet_style: Small emissive sphere (radius 0.1) with matching player color, leaving a short particle trail
visual_spec.hit_effects: Orange spark particles at impact point on hit, red flash on victim mesh
visual_spec.death_effects: Brief mesh scaling pulse then fade, particle burst explosion, respawn fade-in after timer
visual_spec.hud_layout: Leaderboard top-right showing all players sorted by score, crosshair at screen center, kill feed messages bottom-left fading after 4s
